sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: EMERSON ELECTRIC CO의 공시 핵심을 한국어로 요약했습니다.

keyPoints.text: FY2026 2분기 순매출은 44억 3,200만 달러로 전년 동기 대비 성장했으며, 소프트웨어 및 시스템 부문이 성장을 주도했습니다.
조정 주당순이익은 1.48달러로 경영진 기대치를 상회했고, GAAP 주당순이익은 0.86달러였습니다.
기초 주문(underlying orders)이 5% 증가하며 소프트웨어 및 시스템 부문 주도 하에 회복력 있는 수요가 지속됐습니다.

riskChanges.text: 중동 분쟁으로 인해 매출이 일부 영향을 받았으며, 향후 글로벌 분쟁 전개에 따른 불확실성이 지속됩니다.

businessChanges.text: 소프트웨어 및 시스템 부문이 성장 수직 부문에서 지속적인 모멘텀을 보이며 분기 성과를 견인했습니다.
분기 현금 배당금을 주당 0.555달러로 선언했으며 주주환원 기조를 유지했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2분기 조정 세그먼트 EBITA는 12억 4,000만 달러 내지 12억 5,800만 달러 수준으로 마진이 기대치를 초과했습니다.
FY2026 전체 연간 조정 주당순이익 가이던스를 6.45달러~6.55달러로 유지했습니다.
FY2026 연간 잉여현금흐름(Free Cash Flow) 가이던스는 3.5달러~3.6달러 수준으로 제시됐습니다.

watchItems.text: 중동 분쟁 등 글로벌 불확실성 속에서도 소프트웨어 및 시스템 중심의 주문 성장이 하반기까지 유지될지가 핵심입니다.
조정 주당순이익 기준 6.45달러~6.55달러 연간 가이던스 달성 여부와 마진 방어력이 주목 포인트입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 a2026q2release_ex991.htm EX-99.1 Document Exhibit 99.1 Emerson Reports Second Quarter 2026 Results; Updates 2026 Outlook ST. LOUIS, May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Emerson (NYSE: EMR) today reported results for its second quarter ended March 31, 2026 and updated its full year outlook for fiscal 2026. Emerson also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.555 per share of common stock payable June 10, 2026 to stockholders of record on May 15, 2026. (dollars in millions, except per share) 2025 Q2 2026 Q2 Change Underlying Orders 1 5% Net Sales $4,432 $4,562 3% Underlying Sales 2 0.5% Pretax Earnings $629 $793 Margin 14.2% 17.4% 320 bps Adjusted Segment EBITA  Normalized numeric aliases: $0.555 = 0.555달러; $4,432 = 4,432달러; $4,562 = 4,562달러; $629 = 629달러; $793 = 793달러; $1,240 = 1,240달러; $1,258 = 1,258달러; $0.86 = 0.86달러; $1.48 = 1.48달러; $694 = 694달러; $738 = 738달러; 4,432 million = 44억 3,200만 달러; 4,562 million = 45억 6,200만 달러; 1,240 million = 12억 4,000만 달러; 1,258 million = 12억 5,800만 달러.

quarter results reflect our ability to deliver in a dynamic environment," said Emerson President and Chief Executive Officer Lal Karsanbhai. "While sales were impacted by the conflict in the Middle East, margins exceeded expectations, and we achieved strong adjusted earnings per share. Underlying orders were up 5% as we continue to see resilient demand, led by Software & Systems, with sustained momentum in our growth verticals." Karsanbhai continued, "I want to thank our employees and customers in the Middle East for their unwavering commitment and resiliency. As we look ahead, 2026 continues to develop largely as expected with a strong second half, supported by orders momentum and a robust backlog." 2026 Outlook The following tables summarize the fiscal year 2026 guidance framework. As we pivot capital allocation to returning cash to shareholders, the 2026 outlook assumes returning

~$2.2B through ~$1B share repurchases and ~$1.2B of dividends. Guidance figures are approximate. 2026 Q3 2026 Net Sales Growth ~5.5% ~4.5% Underlying Sales Growth ~5% ~3% Earnings Per Share $1.22 - $1.27 $4.79 - $4.89 Amortization of intangibles ~$0.34 ~$1.38 Restructuring and related costs ~$0.06 ~$0.18 Acquisition/divestiture fees and related costs ~$0.02 ~$0.06 Discrete taxes ~$0.01 ~$0.04 Adjusted Earnings Per Share $1.65 - $1.70 $6.45 - $6.55 Operating Cash Flow $4.0B - $4.1B Free Cash Flow $3.5B - $3.6B 1 Underlying orders do not include AspenTech. 2 Underlying sales excludes the impact of currency translation, and significant acquisitions and divestitures. 3 Adjusted segment EBITA represents segment earnings  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.2 = 2.2달러; $1 = 1달러; $1.2 = 1.2달러; $1.22 = 1.22달러; $1.27 = 1.27달러; $4.79 = 4.79달러; $4.89 = 4.89달러; $0.34 = 0.34달러; $0.06 = 0.06달러; $1.65 = 1.65달러; $4.1 = 4.1달러; $6.45 = 6.45달러; $6.55 = 6.55달러; $4.0 = 4.0달러; $3.5 = 3.5달러; $3.6 = 3.6달러.

Emerson undertakes no obligation to update any such statements to reflect new information or later developments. Examples of risks and uncertainties that may cause our actual results or performance to be materially different from those expressed or implied by forward looking statements include the scope, duration and ultimate impacts of the Russia-Ukraine, Middle East and other global conflicts, as well as economic and currency conditions, market demand, pricing, protection of intellectual property, cybersecurity, tariffs, competitive and technological factors, inflation, among others, as set forth in the Company's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K and subsequent reports filed with the SEC.
